title: Permission Request UI Flow
interaction_flow: Caller → UIWindowRequestPermission: call(options={permission, app_uid, app_name, origin, reload_on_success})
get_permission_description → puter.fs: stat({path|uid, consistency:'eventual'}) [if not a standard folder]
get_permission_description → UIWindowRequestPermission: return permission_description (HTML string) or null
UIWindowRequestPermission → UIWindowRequestPermission: resolve(false) if no description (early exit)
create_permission_window → create_window_content: create_window_content(requestingEntity, permission_description) → HTML string
create_permission_window → UIWindow: UIWindow({title:null, app:'request-authorization', body_content:h, width:350, dominant:true, on_close:resolve(false), ...})
setup_window_events → el_window: bind click on '.app-auth-allow' button
setup_window_events → el_window: bind click on '.app-auth-dont-allow' button
[User clicks Allow] → setup_window_events: click event fires
setup_window_events → Browser fetch: POST /auth/grant-user-app {app_uid, origin, permission} with Bearer token
BackendRouter → BackendRouter: validate actor is UserActorType
BackendRouter → validate_fields: validate {app_uid, permission, extra, meta}
BackendRouter → PermissionService: grant_user_app_permission(actor, app_uid, permission, extra, meta)
[User clicks Don't Allow] → setup_window_events: click event fires
key_relationships: UIWindowRequestPermission orchestrates entire flow (async Promise wrapper)
UIWindowRequestPermission depends_on puter.auth.whoami (for permission description resolution)
UIWindowRequestPermission depends_on puter.fs.stat (for fs permission descriptions)
BackendRouter depends_on svc_auth.app_uid_from_origin (when origin provided instead of app_uid)
PermissionService writes to user_to_app_permissions table and audit_user_to_app_permissions table
PermissionService invalidates Redis permission-scan cache on grant
permission_types_handled: fs: filesystem access (read/write/list/see) — resolves standard folders or stat by path/uuid
resolution_outcomes: resolve(true) — user clicked Allow AND POST /auth/grant-user-app succeeded
resolve(false) — user clicked Don't Allow OR window closed OR no permission description found
important_notes: UIWindowRequestPermission is a pure GUI function (no class). Dialog is modal-like (dominant:true, single_instance:true, width:350). Permission string uses colon-delimited format with backslash-C escaping for literal colons. Caller provides {app_uid OR origin, permission, app_name} in options. BackendRouter resolves origin→app_uid via svc_auth if needed. PermissionService skips DB write if permission already exists (idempotent). Audit log always written on new grants.
